summary: Well-structured audio governance pipeline with 3-gate enforcement (consent, moderation, license), Polish-first voice/language defaults, and proper metadata watermarking. Legacy consent backward compatibility is a nice touch. 2 medium + 3 low findings.

findings.title: Metadata extraction relies on duck-typed methods not on port contracts
findings.detail: _extract_tts_metadata() checks _tts.has_method('get_last_request_metadata') and _extract_audio_generation_metadata() checks _audio_generation.has_method('get_last_generation_metadata'). These methods are implemented by the adapters but not declared on TextToSpeechPort or AudioGenerationPort. If a different TTS or audio adapter is used that doesn't implement these methods, the governance service gets empty metadata, bypasses license checks (empty license_id → blocked), and returns a confusing license error instead of a clear 'metadata unavailable' error.
findings.recommendation: Add get_last_request_metadata() -> Dictionary to TextToSpeechPort and get_last_generation_metadata() -> Dictionary to AudioGenerationPort with default {} returns. This formalizes the contract and makes governance work predictably with any conforming adapter.

findings.title: Voice role resolution uses duck-typed method on TTS adapter
findings.detail: generate_narration() calls _tts.has_method('resolve_voice_for_role') at line 73. This is an adapter-specific method not on TextToSpeechPort. If a conforming TTS adapter doesn't implement it, voice_id falls back to the raw role string (e.g., 'narration') which may not map to a valid provider voice_id, causing silent failures in synthesis.
findings.recommendation: Either add resolve_voice_for_role() to TextToSpeechPort with a default pass-through, or handle the fallback explicitly by mapping common role names to safe defaults within the governance service itself.

findings.title: allow_publish defaults to true when provider metadata omits the field
findings.detail: _augment_metadata reads result.get('allow_publish', true) which defaults to true. If a provider adapter returns metadata without an allow_publish field, the audio is implicitly marked as publishable. The safety-first default should be false (deny publishing unless explicitly allowed).
findings.recommendation: Change the default to false: result.get('allow_publish', false) to follow the fail-closed pattern used elsewhere in the engine.

findings.title: Language override mixes kid profile data with parent policy control
findings.detail: _resolve_audio_language reads actor.language directly and then checks actor.preferences for 'allow_non_polish_audio'. This couples the governance service to the PlayerProfile internal structure. If a kid has a non-Polish language preference set but their parent hasn't explicitly allowed it, the service correctly falls back to pl-PL. However, the logic would be cleaner using a dedicated consent/preference check rather than inspecting actor fields.
findings.recommendation: Consider using the consent port with a 'non_polish_audio' consent type, or extract language policy into a separate method on LocalizationPolicyPort.

findings.title: Safety event decision_id uses non-cryptographic hash
findings.detail: Same recurring pattern: absi(seed.hash()) for decision_id generation. Collisions between different policy_rule/actor/trigger combinations would deduplicate events in audit systems.
findings.recommendation: Incorporate a monotonic counter or timestamp for uniqueness.

positive_observations: Clean 3-gate governance: consent → moderation → license checks before any audio output
Polish-first enforcement: non-Polish output requires explicit parent preference override
Legacy consent backward compatibility: cloud_tts accepted alongside cloud_audio_generation
Non-lyrical enforcement by default for ambient audio (bez wokalu tag)
Separate playback_allowed and publish_allowed flags for different consumption contexts
AI watermark metadata (ai_audio tag) on all generated audio for downstream identification
License allowlist pattern with configurable approved_license_ids
SafetyInterventionTriggeredEvent emission for all governance blocks with specific policy rules
Parent-only bypass for consent gate — parents always have implicit consent
13 contract assertions covering consent gate, moderation gate, license gate, and event emission
